reasoning_details.text:
We need to translate the given markdown content into Hebrew, preserving code blocks, MDX components, headings, etc. Also need to adjust relative URLs to have ../ prefix. The original relative URLs: ./hero-wide.webp, ./square-200.webp, ./square-200.webp, ./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg, ./hero-concept-8-drawers.webp. Need to prefix with ../ (since they are relative). So change to ../hero-wide.webp etc. Also need to preserve frontmatter, translating only reader-facing values: language, title, subTitle, cover_alt, cover_credit. The frontmatter currently has title, subTitle, date, modified, tags, category, draft, popularity, social_image, cover_full_width, cover_mobile, cover_icon. No language field currently. We need to add language: "Hebrew"? The instruction: If a language field exists, set it exactly to "Hebrew". It does not exist, but maybe we need to add? It says translate only reader-facing frontmatter values: language, title, subTitle, cover_alt, and cover_credit. So we need to translate title and subTitle. Keep other keys unchanged. Should we add language field? Not required unless exists. So we keep frontmatter as is, but translate title and subTitle values to Hebrew. Also need to keep key order. So title first, subTitle second. We'll translate them. Title: "It's Time for llm:// Connection Strings" => Hebrew translation: "הגיע הזמן למחרוזות חיבור llm://". Might be "זה הזמן למחרוזות חיבור llm://". Keep similar tone. Let's do: "הגיע הזמן למחרוזות חיבור llm://". SubTitle: "Simplify Model & Provider Config with `llm://` URLs" => "פשטו את תצורת המודל והספק עם כתובות `llm://`". We'll translate. Now the body: translate prose, preserve code blocks unchanged. Also need to translate blockquote text, etc. Also need to translate the footnote note text. Also need to translate the inline code spans unchanged. Also need to adjust relative URLs in markdown image tags: ![the parts of a LLM connection string](./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg) => change to ../inline-url-diagram-dark.svg. Similarly for hero-concept-8-drawers.webp. Also need to adjust any other relative URLs: the social_image etc are not in body. The frontmatter cover paths are not changed? They are not reader-facing. So keep as is. Now translate the prose. Keep headings levels. Let's go through content. First blockquote: **Update:** This article led to an [Internet-Draft for the `llm://` URI scheme](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-levy-llm-uri-scheme/). => translate: **עדכון:** מאמר זה הוביל ל[טיוטת אינטרנט עבור סכמת ה-URI `llm://`](https://...). Keep link unchanged. Then paragraph: Remember the bad old days when connecting to a database meant juggling a miscellaneous grab-bag of environment variables? => translate. Continue. Need to keep code block unchanged. Then "One string. Everything you need. Universally parseable. Portable. Dare I say... beautiful?" translate. "So why are we treating LLMs like it's 1999?" translate. Heading ## The Env Var Explosion => translate heading text. Paragraphs. Need to translate "It's not just ugly; it's friction." etc. Also note footnote: ~~stole~~ borrowed the DB URL idea? Keep strikethrough? The original uses ~~stole~~. We'll keep same. Heading ## Introducing LLM Connection Strings => translate. Paragraph with code block. Then "---\n
\n\n### Anatomy of an LLM Connection String" => translate heading. Image alt text: "the parts of a LLM connection string" => translate alt text. Then description. Heading ## Need auth? Great, add it. => translate. Paragraph. Note block: *Note: Yes, putting credentials in URLs can be a security risk...* => translate. Heading ## Resiliency? Why the hell not. => translate. Paragraph. Code block. Note about s. Blockquote. Heading ## Alternative Formats => translate. Paragraph. Code block. List items: translate bullet points. Blockquote with AI response: translate. Heading ## The Verdict => translate. Paragraph. Image alt: "a messy env var drawer" => translate. Footnote comment: {/* ¹ Yes, I know that `URI` is more correct than `URL`. If you are pedantic enough to actually care about that distinction, please go touch grass. */} => translate comment text but keep comment syntax. Now ensure we preserve blank lines.
